title: RapidMeta Gastroenterology | Tofacitinib in Moderate-to-Severe Ulcerative Colitis (OCTAVE) v1.0
hero_h2: Tofacitinib in Moderate-to-Severe Ulcerative Colitis (OCTAVE Programme)

pico.pop: Adults with moderate-to-severe ulcerative colitis (Mayo score 6-12 with endoscopic subscore >=2; OCTAVE Induction 1/2: prior conventional or anti-TNF therapy failure; OCTAVE Sustain: responders to induction)
pico.int: Tofacitinib 10 mg PO BID (induction phase, 8 weeks) or 5 mg / 10 mg PO BID (maintenance phase, 52 weeks)
pico.out: Clinical remission (Mayo total <=2 with no individual subscore >1, including rectal-bleeding subscore =0; primary at week 8 in induction trials, week 52 in OCTAVE Sustain)
pico.subgroup: Prior anti-TNF exposure, baseline Mayo, concomitant corticosteroid, age, sex

real_data_entries.name: OCTAVE Induction 1
real_data_entries.group: Tofacitinib 10 mg BID vs placebo, 8-week induction in moderate-severe UC; primary clinical remission at week 8 (Mayo)
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Sandborn WJ et al. NEJM 2017;376:1723-1736 (OCTAVE Induction 1 + 2 + Sustain).

real_data_entries.name: OCTAVE Induction 2
real_data_entries.group: Tofacitinib 10 mg BID vs placebo, 8-week induction in moderate-severe UC; primary clinical remission at week 8 (Mayo)
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Sandborn WJ et al. NEJM 2017;376:1723-1736 (OCTAVE Induction 2).

real_data_entries.name: OCTAVE Sustain
real_data_entries.group: Tofacitinib 10 mg BID vs placebo, 52-week maintenance in induction-responders to moderate-severe UC; primary clinical remission at week 52
real_data_entries.snippet: Source: Sandborn WJ et al. NEJM 2017;376:1723-1736 (OCTAVE Sustain maintenance).
